source_request: Plan implementation for GitHub issue #5 in /Users/brian/dev/github/kontourai/kontour-console: add a dependency-free Kontour emitter plus local and composite sink skeleton.

criteria.description: A test emits one control event through CompositeSink([LocalFileSink, InMemorySink]) and verifies the same event.id reaches both sinks.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: assertions over the local JSONL event line and InMemorySink accepted record/result showing identical event.id.

criteria.description: A test emits one projection snapshot through the local sink and verifies it can be read by the existing inspector/loader.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: emitted .kontour projection JSON is loaded by loadProjectionSnapshots or inspectFixtures with no validation errors.

criteria.description: Per-sink delivery result includes sink id/name, status, and error details when simulated failure occurs.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: failing sink test asserts sinkId, sinkRole/name, outcome/status failed, recordId, recordKind, errorCode, safeMessage, observedAt, and visible sibling results.

criteria.description: Security/scope scan finds no child_process, command execution, URL open/fetch, or network imports.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: no implementation/test usage of forbidden APIs; any doc-only mentions are confirmed as prohibitions or future non-implemented roles.

criteria.description: README or docs show local+future API fanout concept without implementing the API.
criteria.notes: Expected evidence: usage snippet shows LocalFileSink plus CompositeSink and clearly labels API fanout as future-only.

goal_fit.summary: Pending execution and verification: the implementation should provide a dependency-free local-first emitter/sink skeleton preserving issue #4 delivery and identity contracts.
